metadata.scheme_name: Establishment and Operation of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) Laboratories

raw_data.details: The scheme “Establishment and Operation of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) Laboratories” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to establish STEM laboratories in schools across Uttarakhand to provide students of classes 6 to 12 with hands-on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics learning opportunities and strengthen their practical understanding of scientific concepts.
raw_data.benefits: Under this, 41 STEM laboratories have been established across the state to strengthen practical science education for secondary and higher secondary level students and to provide hands-on learning activities for students of classes 6 to 12, enabling them to conduct science-related experiments in these labs.
raw_data.eligibility: Students must be studying in classes 6 to degree level in a school in Uttarakhand. Students from schools with a STEM laboratory, as well as those from nearby schools, are eligible to use the STEM laboratories.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineAll students of schools with STEM laboratories can conduct experiments in the laboratories established in their schools, while students from schools without laboratories can avail of this benefit by visiting nearby schools that have STEM labs.
raw_data.documents_required: Documents required as per the guidelines of the respective schools that have STEM laboratories

To know more please visit Check EligibilityUttarakhandEstablishment and Operation of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) LaboratoriesEngineeringLaboratoriesMathematicsScienceSTEMStudentTechnologyDetailsThe scheme “Establishment and Operation of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) Laboratories” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to establish STEM laboratories in schools across Uttarakhand to provide students of classes 6 to 12 with hands-on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics learning opportunities and strengthen their practical understanding of scientific concepts. BenefitsUnder this, 41 STEM laboratories have been established across the state to strengthen practical science education for secondary and higher secondary level students and to provide hands-on learning activities for students of classes 6 to 12, enabling them to conduct science-related experiments in these labs. EligibilityStudents must be studying in classes 6 to degree level in a school in Uttarakhand. Students from schools with a STEM laboratory, as well as those from nearby schools, are eligible to use the STEM laboratories. Application ProcessOfflineAll students of schools with STEM laboratories can conduct experiments in the laboratories established in their schools, while students from schools without laboratories can avail of this benefit by visiting nearby schools that have STEM labs. Documents RequiredDocuments required as per the guidelines of the respective schools that have STEM laboratoriesFrequently Asked QuestionsWhich students are eligible to use the STEM laboratories? Students of classes 6 to degree level studying in schools in Uttarakhand are eligible. How many STEM laboratories have been established under the scheme? A total of 41 STEM laboratories have been established across the state. Can students from nearby schools use the STEM labs? Yes, students from nearby schools without laboratories can also avail of the facilities by contacting schools with STEM labs. What activities are conducted in the STEM laboratories? Students conduct science-related and engineering-related experiments in these laboratories. Can degree-level students conduct experiments in the labs? Yes. How can students from schools without STEM labs access the facilities? They can contact nearby schools that have STEM laboratories to conduct experiments. Sources And References[Guidelines (Page No. 118)](https://uk.gov.in/department92/library_file/file-04-12-2023-06-02-23.pdf)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: Which students are eligible to use the STEM laboratories?
faqs.answer: Students of classes 6 to degree level studying in schools in Uttarakhand are eligible.

faqs.question: How many STEM laboratories have been established under the scheme?
faqs.answer: A total of 41 STEM laboratories have been established across the state.

faqs.question: Can students from nearby schools use the STEM labs?
faqs.answer: Yes, students from nearby schools without laboratories can also avail of the facilities by contacting schools with STEM labs.

faqs.question: What activities are conducted in the STEM laboratories?
faqs.answer: Students conduct science-related and engineering-related experiments in these laboratories.

faqs.question: Can degree-level students conduct experiments in the labs?
How can students from schools without STEM labs access the facilities?
faqs.answer: They can contact nearby schools that have STEM laboratories to conduct experiments.
